purpose: Expose a new getPendingDirectInvites(groupId) context method that faithfully reads the S1 pending-direct-invite marker store, and provide regression evidence that the approve-join-request path is structurally excluded from ever writing that marker (AC-MARKER-4).

public_api.name: MarmotContextValue.getPendingDirectInvites
public_api.description: New context method (4-site pattern). Dynamic-imports loadPendingDirectInviteMarkers from pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts at the boundary and returns its result unmodified — no transformation, filtering, or caching layer. DEFAULT_MARMOT fallback (outside a provider) resolves an empty Set, never undefined or a throw, so S9's unconditional per-load caller cannot crash before the provider is ready.

dependencies_allowed: app/src/lib/marmot/pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts (loadPendingDirectInviteMarkers export, consumed via dynamic import at the useCallback boundary only, per the established cancelPendingInvitation/isPendingMember precedent)
dependencies_forbidden: no static top-level import of pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts's loadPendingDirectInviteMarkers (dynamic-import-at-boundary is the fixed convention for this method; a static top-level import already exists in this file for clearPendingDirectInvite (S4) and clearPendingDirectInvitesForGroup (S3's leaveGroup boundary uses dynamic import) — this story's read method follows the read-boundary precedent, not S4's write precedent)
no new runtime guard inside approveJoinRequestImpl to 'prevent' a marker write — the exclusion is structural (insertion-point choice) and this story verifies that fact, it does not add a guard
test_targets: app/tests/unit/marmot/approveJoinRequestImpl.test.ts (AC-MARKER-4 regression: approveJoinRequestImpl never causes a marker to exist for the approved pubkey)
